description: Insect protein bar as novel food: authorisation, safety assessment, labelling, traceability

pipeline_inputs.query_text: insect protein novel food authorisation labelling requirements

ground_truth_requirements.description: Only novel foods authorised and included in the Union list may be placed on the EU market
Novel food must not pose a safety risk to human health, must not mislead consumers, and must not be nutritionally disadvantageous compared to the food it replaces
ground_truth_requirements.notes: May be extracted as authorisation rather than safety_assessment

ground_truth_requirements.description: Application for novel food authorisation must include name, description, production process, composition, scientific safety evidence, and proposed labelling conditions
Novel food must comply with specific conditions of use and labelling requirements set in the Union list entry
Post-market monitoring requirements may be imposed where specified in the authorisation decision; operator must immediately inform Commission of new information on safety
ground_truth_requirements.notes: if_applicable or notification priority also acceptable

ground_truth_requirements.description: Food business operators must establish traceability at all stages and be able to identify suppliers and recipients of food products
Food shall not be placed on the market if it is unsafe (injurious to health or unfit for human consumption)
Mandatory food information: name, ingredients list, allergens, net quantity, date marking, storage conditions, nutrition declaration
A novel food may only be placed on the EU market if it is included in the Union list of authorised novel foods established by this regulation
A consultation request on novel food status must be submitted electronically to the recipient Member State with specified information
A notification on novel food status must include the name and description of the food, its composition, and evidence of significant consumption history
The summary dossier in a novel food authorisation application must set out reasons why the novel food does not pose a safety risk
Toxicological studies submitted in novel food applications must be conducted in facilities complying with good laboratory practice
A notification to modify conditions of use, specifications, labelling or monitoring requirements for an authorised novel food must include specified information
An application to modify conditions of use, specifications, labelling requirements, or monitoring conditions must be submitted with supporting evidence
The labelling, advertising and presentation of food must not mislead consumers as to characteristics, composition, nature, or properties
Any food intended for supply to the final consumer or mass caterers must be accompanied by food information
Regulation 1169/2011 on food information applies to all food business operators at all stages of the food chain
